name: Kono's Northshore
tagline: Haleiwa breakfast spot famous for 12-hour kalua pork in burritos and benedicts — fuel for a North Shore surf day.
mustOrder: kalua pork breakfast burrito — 12-hour smoked, the signature
Hawaiian plate with rice and mac salad
parking: Small lot at 66-250 Kamehameha Hwy; street parking in Haleiwa town
bestTime: Pre-surf breakfast by 8am. Weekends get a line by 9am
talkStory: Kono's is the surf crowd's breakfast spot in Haleiwa. The star is the 12-hour smoked kalua pork that goes into everything — burritos, eggs benny, plate lunches. It's the kind of place where you order at the counter, grab a seat on the lanai, and watch the North Shore wake up. Not trying to be fancy, just trying to feed surfers properly. The burritos are massive and under $15, which is almost unheard of in Haleiwa these days.
